text: Meetings are the only known technology that lets a dozen people simultaneously agree that something must be done while ensuring that none of them is the one who has to do it. They run on a peculiar conservation law: the number of action items leaving a meeting is always smaller than the number of hours spent generating them, and the difference is converted into a follow-up meeting to discuss why the action items weren't completed. The truly enduring ones achieve a kind of immortality, recurring on the calendar long after anyone remembers what they were meant to decide, attended out of the dim fear that the one time you skip will be the time they finally get to the point.
